args:
Identify and rank the most impactful benchmarks, leaderboards, and "arenas" for evaluating AI DEEP-RESEARCH agents — systems that autonomously search the web and academic sources, then write long-form, fully-cited research reports (competitors: OpenAI Deep Research, Google Gemini Deep Research, Perplexity Deep Research, xAI, Anthropic). As of 2026.

CONTEXT / WHY: We operate our own deep-research agent and currently measure ourselves only with DeepResearch-Bench (its RACE report-quality score and FACT citation-support score). We want the FULL competitive scoreboard landscape so we can (a) prioritize which benchmarks to optimize for, and (b) know where an external team can actually deploy/submit a score for public credibility and visibility.

FOR EACH benchmark / leaderboard / arena, report:
1. Name, the organization or people who run it, and its canonical URL.
2. What it measures — task type (open-web browsing QA, long-form cited report quality, citation faithfulness/hallucination, tool-use, multi-hop reasoning) and the exact metric(s) it reports.
3. How scores are produced and whether an OUTSIDE team can be scored: is there a public leaderboard, an automatic LLM judge, a formal submission/PR process, a held-out test set, or head-to-head human/arena voting? Is the harness open-source and self-runnable?
4. Impact / visibility — who currently appears on it (which labs/products), whether major labs cite it in launches or papers, and roughly how much industry attention it has.
5. Known strengths, limitations, and how gameable the metric is.

SPECIFICALLY INVESTIGATE AND DISAMBIGUATE:
- "arena.ai" — what is it exactly? Distinguish clearly from LMArena / lmarena.ai (formerly Chatbot Arena / LMSYS) and from any separate "Deep Research Arena". Report what actually lives at arena.ai.
- LMArena / Chatbot Arena: does it have a deep-research, web-search, or "search arena" category where a full research agent (not just a chat model) can compete? How does submission work?
- DeepResearch Bench (RACE + FACT) — who maintains it, is there a public leaderboard, how do you submit.
- GAIA (general assistant benchmark), BrowseComp and BrowseComp-Plus (OpenAI), Humanity's Last Exam (HLE), FRAMES, SimpleQA, WebArena / WebVoyager / Mind2Web, ResearchQA, DeepConsult, and any academic "Deep Research Arena" or agent-report evaluation.
- Any venue specifically designed to score or vote on autonomous deep-research REPORTS head-to-head (the closest analog to an "arena" for our product).

DELIVERABLE: a RANKED shortlist (most impactful first) of the venues/benchmarks that BOTH (a) carry the most credibility/visibility in the field, AND (b) actually allow an external deep-research system to be scored or submitted. For each, give the risk/effort of getting on it. End with a clear TOP RECOMMENDATION of the 2-3 venues we should target first and exactly why, plus a note on which are pure internal-measurement tools versus public leaderboards we can climb.

summary: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report.

phases.title: Scope
phases.detail: Decompose question (from args) into 5 search angles

phases.title: Search
phases.detail: 5 parallel WebSearch agents, one per angle

phases.title: Fetch
phases.detail: URL-dedup, fetch top 15 sources, extract falsifiable claims

phases.title: Verify
phases.detail: 3-vote adversarial verification per claim (need 2/3 refutes to kill)

phases.title: Synthesize
phases.detail: Merge semantic dupes, rank by confidence, cite sources
